name: Lebanese Stuffed Grape Leaves
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the rice in a colander and rinse 3 times with cold water.
2. Combine the salt, pepper, allspice, and cinnamon, then sprinkle over the rice, stirring to mix well.
3. Add butter and lamb to the rice and mix thoroughly.
4. Place grape leaves in a large bowl and cover with boiling water to soften. Remove and drain in a colander.
5. Cool the grape leaves.
6. To stuff the leaves, place a small amount of the rice and meat mixture on each leaf. Begin at the stem end, roll the leaf up over the filling, folding the sides of the leaf in halfway toward the center. Tuck the leaf tightly by the time you reach the tip.
7. Repeat with the remaining leaves and filling.
8. Cover the bottom of a large pan with a few grape leaves and lemon slices.
9. In neat rows, arrange the stuffed grape leaves on top of the lemon slices.
10. Invert a heavy dish on top of the grape leaves so that the bottom of the dish is pressing down on them.
11. Add water to the pan, bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer on low for about 35 minutes, or until the stuffed grape leaves are tender.
